signals.headline: Cavaliers lead Raptors 2-0 after Game 2 win, extending playoff dominance to 14-2 all-time | NO series win for Raptors |
signals.raw:
Cavaliers lead Raptors 2-0 after Game 2 win, extending playoff dominance to 14-2 all-time | NO series win for Raptors | HIGH confidence**[1][2][5]

Cleveland Cavaliers defeated Toronto Raptors 115-105 in Game 2 on Monday, with Donovan Mitchell (30 points), James Harden (28 points), and Evan Mobley (25 points, 8 rebounds) leading to a 2-0 series lead; series shifts to Toronto for Game 3 on Thursday
